Members who pay this fee gain physical proximity to a sitting US president at social events. CREW documented 55 foreign officials from 21 countries visiting Trump properties in year one. The fee structure has no disclosure requirements: foreign nationals, foreign-government-linked entities, and domestic influence-seekers can all join without public record.

incidents.title: Liberation Day tariffs and pause — suspected insider trading and market manipulation

incidents.title: Trump fires 17 Inspectors General in overnight purge
incidents.summary: On the night of January 24-25, 2025, Trump fired 17 Senate-confirmed Inspectors General via email with no notice. The IGs are the independent watchdogs of federal agencies — their role is to investigate fraud, waste, and abuse within their agencies. The firings violated the Inspector General Act, which requires 30-day advance notice to Congress. A federal judge later called it 'obvious' that the firings were unlawful. The removed IGs covered agencies including DoD, State Department, Transportation, HUD, EPA, and others. Congress was not notified in advance. At least some IGs were reinstated pending litigation, but most were not. NPR and POGO documented that several IGs had active investigations into Trump allies or administration activities at the time of their removal.

incidents.summary: Trump Media & Technology Group (TMTG), which owns Truth Social, trades publicly on Nasdaq under DJT. Trump personally holds approximately 60% of shares, worth hundreds of millions of dollars depending on share price. Since January 20, 2025, TMTG has announced expansion deals including a financial services platform (Truth.Fi), plans for streaming services, and licensing agreements with foreign partners. CREW and ethics organizations have noted that: (1) foreign nationals can buy TMTG shares on the open market, indirectly transferring money to Trump while he is president; (2) Trump's public posts on Truth Social directly affect TMTG's share price and his own net worth; (3) regulatory decisions by the SEC and FTC, which Trump controls through appointments, affect TMTG's business. TMTG also announced plans to launch ETFs and investment products through Truth.Fi, creating another channel for foreign money to flow to Trump-controlled entities.
